peer_exemplars.reasoning: Moderate support from high GAL-TAN participatory democracy preference (64.58) and free-spirit autonomy (66.71), tempered by very low agreeableness (16.2) and skepticism of collective deliberation.

High openness and participatory democracy preference (60.78) drive moderate support for citizen assemblies, but low trust in institutions tempers enthusiasm.

High openness, very low agreeableness, and strong GAL-TAN green/alternative/libertarian participatory democracy preference combine with ambivalent trust in formal institutions to produce mild support for an innovative, inclusive but potentially inefficient democratic mechanism.
High libertarianism and anti-authoritarianism drive support for participatory democracy, but low openness and conventional moral stage temper enthusiasm.

High agreeableness and very low openness make him mildly supportive of participatory democracy in principle, but his strong authoritarian submission and low internal efficacy temper enthusiasm for citizen-led bodies.

Very low openness and low GAL-TAN green-libertarian scores (3.84%) indicate deep skepticism toward participatory democratic innovations, while high authoritarian submission (71.55%) and conventionalism (84.84%) favor established hierarchical decision-making over citizen-led assemblies.
Low openness and high authoritarian submission temper enthusiasm for participatory democracy, but high extraversion and civic engagement create mild support for citizen input.
